hookCapture.patchTarget: Parse-postfix (OK: Command.Parse, OK: Command.Parse, OK: CommandLineConfiguration.Parse, OK: CommandLineConfiguration.Parse, OK: CommandLineParser.Parse, OK: CommandLineParser.Parse, OK: CommandLineConfiguration.Invoke, OK: CommandLineConfiguration.Invoke, OK: ParseResult.Invoke, OK: ParseErrorAction.Invoke, OK: HelpAction.Invoke, OK: CommandLineConfiguration.InvokeAsync, OK: CommandLineConfiguration.InvokeAsync, OK: ParseResult.InvokeAsync, OK: RootCommand.ctor(String))

options.name: --help
options.description: Show help and usage information

options.name: --version
options.description: Show version information

commands.name: client-server
commands.description: Start a .NET application Diagnostics Server routing local IPC server <--> remote TCP client. Router is configured using an IPC client (connecting diagnostic tool IPC server) and a TCP/IP server (accepting runtime TCP client).

options.name: --ipc-client
options.description: The diagnostic tool diagnostics server ipc address (--diagnostic-port argument). Router connects diagnostic tool ipc server when establishing a new route between runtime and diagnostic tool.

options.name: --tcp-server
options.description: The router TCP/IP address using format [host]:[port]. Router can bind one (127.0.0.1, [::1], 0.0.0.0, [::], ipv4 address, ipv6 address, hostname) or all (*) interfaces. Launch runtime using DOTNET_DiagnosticPorts environment variable connecting router TCP server during startup.

options.name: --runtime-timeout
options.description: Automatically shutdown router if no runtime connects to it before specified timeout (seconds).If not specified, router won't trigger an automatic shutdown.

options.name: --verbose
options.description: Enable verbose logging (none|critical|error|warning|info|debug|trace)

options.name: --forward-port
options.description: Enable port forwarding, values Android|iOS for TcpClient and only Android for TcpServer. For Android, optionally set $ANDROID_HOME to a valid Android SDK path.

options.name: --block-signals
options.description: Blocks specified signals, currently SIGINT and SIGQUIT can be disabled, each signal is separated with ;

commands.name: server-server
commands.description: Start a .NET application Diagnostics Server routing local IPC client <--> remote TCP client. Router is configured using an IPC server (connecting to by diagnostic tools) and a TCP/IP server (accepting runtime TCP client).

options.name: --ipc-server
options.description: The diagnostics server ipc address to route. Router accepts ipc connections from diagnostic tools establishing a new route between runtime and diagnostic tool. If not specified router will use default ipc diagnostics server path.

options.name: --tcp-server
options.description: The router TCP/IP address using format [host]:[port]. Router can bind one (127.0.0.1, [::1], 0.0.0.0, [::], ipv4 address, ipv6 address, hostname) or all (*) interfaces. Launch runtime using DOTNET_DiagnosticPorts environment variable connecting router TCP server during startup.

options.name: --runtime-timeout
options.description: Automatically shutdown router if no runtime connects to it before specified timeout (seconds).If not specified, router won't trigger an automatic shutdown.

options.name: --verbose
options.description: Enable verbose logging (none|critical|error|warning|info|debug|trace)

options.name: --forward-port
options.description: Enable port forwarding, values Android|iOS for TcpClient and only Android for TcpServer. For Android, optionally set $ANDROID_HOME to a valid Android SDK path.

options.name: --block-signals
options.description: Blocks specified signals, currently SIGINT and SIGQUIT can be disabled, each signal is separated with ;

commands.name: server-client
commands.description: Start a .NET application Diagnostics Server routing local IPC client <--> remote TCP server. Router is configured using an IPC server (connecting to by diagnostic tools) and a TCP/IP client (connecting runtime TCP server).

options.name: --ipc-server
options.description: The diagnostics server ipc address to route. Router accepts ipc connections from diagnostic tools establishing a new route between runtime and diagnostic tool. If not specified router will use default ipc diagnostics server path.

options.name: --tcp-client
options.description: The runtime TCP/IP address using format [host]:[port]. Router can can connect 127.0.0.1, [::1], ipv4 address, ipv6 address, hostname addresses.Launch runtime using DOTNET_DiagnosticPorts environment variable to setup listener.

options.name: --runtime-timeout
options.description: Automatically shutdown router if no runtime connects to it before specified timeout (seconds).If not specified, router won't trigger an automatic shutdown.

options.name: --verbose
options.description: Enable verbose logging (none|critical|error|warning|info|debug|trace)

options.name: --forward-port
options.description: Enable port forwarding, values Android|iOS for TcpClient and only Android for TcpServer. For Android, optionally set $ANDROID_HOME to a valid Android SDK path.

options.name: --block-signals
options.description: Blocks specified signals, currently SIGINT and SIGQUIT can be disabled, each signal is separated with ;

commands.name: server-websocket
commands.description: Starts a .NET application Diagnostic Server routing local IPC client <--> remote WebSocket client. Router is configured using an IPC server (connecting to by diagnostic tools) and a WebSocket server (accepting runtime WebSocket client).

options.name: --ipc-server
options.description: The diagnostics server ipc address to route. Router accepts ipc connections from diagnostic tools establishing a new route between runtime and diagnostic tool. If not specified router will use default ipc diagnostics server path.

options.name: --web-socket
options.description: The router WebSocket address using format ws://[host]:[port]/[path] or wss://[host]:[port]/[path]. Launch app with WasmExtraConfig property specifying diagnostic_options with a server connect_url

options.name: --runtime-timeout
options.description: Automatically shutdown router if no runtime connects to it before specified timeout (seconds).If not specified, router won't trigger an automatic shutdown.

options.name: --verbose
options.description: Enable verbose logging (none|critical|error|warning|info|debug|trace)

options.name: --block-signals
options.description: Blocks specified signals, currently SIGINT and SIGQUIT can be disabled, each signal is separated with ;

commands.name: ios-sim
commands.description: Start a .NET application Diagnostics Server routing local IPC server <--> iOS Simulator. Router is configured using an IPC server (connecting to by diagnostic tools) and a TCP/IP server (accepting runtime TCP client).

options.name: --runtime-timeout
options.description: Automatically shutdown router if no runtime connects to it before specified timeout (seconds).If not specified, router won't trigger an automatic shutdown.

options.name: --verbose
options.description: Enable verbose logging (none|critical|error|warning|info|debug|trace)

options.name: --info
options.description: Print info on how to use current dotnet-dsrouter instance with application and diagnostic tooling.

options.name: --block-signals
options.description: Blocks specified signals, currently SIGINT and SIGQUIT can be disabled, each signal is separated with ;

options.name: --parentprocess
options.description: If dsrouter was launched from dotnet-trace or another .NET diagnostic tool, contains the parent process ID and name: --parentprocess pid:name

commands.name: ios
commands.description: Start a .NET application Diagnostics Server routing local IPC server <--> iOS Device over usbmux. Router is configured using an IPC server (connecting to by diagnostic tools) and a TCP/IP client (connecting runtime TCP server over usbmux).

options.name: --runtime-timeout
options.description: Automatically shutdown router if no runtime connects to it before specified timeout (seconds).If not specified, router won't trigger an automatic shutdown.

options.description: Print info on how to use current dotnet-dsrouter instance with application and diagnostic tooling.

options.name: --parentprocess
options.description: If dsrouter was launched from dotnet-trace or another .NET diagnostic tool, contains the parent process ID and name: --parentprocess pid:name
